programmes.show_description: Dara O Briain hosts the potent panel show that's a heady mocktail of biting satire and surreal stand-up. With Hugh Dennis, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones and Josh Widdicombe.
The fabulous Jason Manford is your host for a special festive edition of the show as he introduces comedians Maisie Adam and Nabil Abdulrashid to the stage.
Underground and Underwater: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Zoe Lyons, Dan Tiernan, Josh Widdicombe and Alan Davies for an underground and underwater episode.
Pregnancy Test: Expect more hilarity with another edition of the hilarious comedy. When Johnny finds a pregnancy test in the rubbish, everyone assumes that Alexis is pregnant as a result.
Asbestos Fest: More comedy with the calamity-cursed Rose clan. Moira headlines a charity fundraiser, Johnny and Stevie take on a new worker, and David and Patrick contend with shoplifters.
Girls' Night: More quality time with the smash-hit sitcom from across the pond. Moira tries to push Alexis into the dating scene, and Patrick and Stevie confront David's inflexibility.
RIP Moira Rose: Stone-cold comedy from the most interestingly-named sitcom town ever. A rumour that Moira is dead circulates online, and Roland asks Johnny to be his son's godfather.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping: Richard checks out a robotic shopping assistant with farmer Jimmy Doherty and visits a busy department store to see a 3D virtual mirror with actress Alison Steadman.
Food Festivals: Another helping of top British grub with the bikers. The duo take a look at all the food festivals in the calendar that allow the British to indulge.
The boys embark on a mission of Biblical proportions: to find the source of the Nile using three ageing motors to negotiate traffic, mudslides, and crocodile-infested waters.
First-ever episode of the much-loved sitcom that launched Smithy onto the scene! After being phone buddies for months, Gavin and Stacey arrange a rendezvous in London.
When the couple have their first lovers' tiff, Gavin is so beside himself with worry that he drives all the way to Barry Island to make amends.
The couple announce their engagement and the families unite for a party in Billericay, but they are unprepared for a bombshell that awaits them.
The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a trip to the church to hear the banns being read, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret.
The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding.
The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Stacey's brother, while Nessa debates whether to share her big secret.
Paul and Bob return to Scotland to fish for salmon once more, this time on the mighty River Tweed after a previous last-ditch attempt to catch the fish on the River Tay.
Paul takes Bob on a trip down memory lane and back to the River Lea in Hertfordshire - where Paul used to come as a teenager with his mates - and welcomes a special guest.
Paul and Bob head to Norfolk and attempt to ensnare a big pike - something that has eluded them so far on their fishing trips - before Bob reveals a hidden trick to Paul.
San Francisco to Monterey: Rick Stein begins a journey from northern California to Mexico - in this opening episode, Rick is in San Francisco to taste legendary dishes like the hangtown fry.
Monterey to San Diego: Rick Stein travels down the Californian coastline immortalised by John Steinbeck, past citrus groves and vineyards that inspired one of his favourite films, and into LA.
Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material with guests including Alex Jones, Will Mellor, Naga Munchetty and Gabby Logan.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Our survey says... that former Family Fortunes host Les Dennis is joining actor Charlie Brooks, comedian Darren Harriott, and TV presenter Melinda Messenger on this edition of the show.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Guest host Gary Neville and panellists Maisie Adam and Richard Madeley join Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.
New. Frankie helps make sense of the bewildering times we live in with the help of Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of guests.
Underground and Underwater: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Zoe Lyons, Dan Tiernan, Josh Widdicombe and Alan Davies for an underground and underwater episode.
Dara O Briain chairs the popular satirical show that's only very loosely a quiz and more a good excuse for some top-draw craic. With Ed Byrne, Sara Pascoe and Rob Beckett.
Surely royalty wouldn't lie? Danny Dyer is among the famous fibbers in the award-winning comedy panel show. Jon Richardson, Joe Lycett and Moira Stuart also adopt poker faces.
Ulex: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Bridget Christie, Rosie Jones, Cariad Lloyd and Alan Davies to find out about ulex and other obscure 'U' words.
Frankie helps make sense of the bewildering times we live in with the help of Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of guests.
Open Mic: Hit sitcom that's packed with witticism-packed weirdness. Patrick hosts an open mic night to lure customers into the store, and Alexis works on a marketing strategy for the motel.
The Barbecue: Hot comedy from across the pond. The Rose family throws a barbecue to celebrate David and Patrick's four-month anniversary, but an unexpected visitor threatens everything.
The Jazzaguy: Sparklingly showy silliness with the hit sitcom. Johnny is warmly embraced by the Jazzagals, David and Stevie take a brief holiday, and Alexis goes out with Ted's rival.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Game: The bikers continue their culinary journey through time as they celebrate British food. Time for some meaty dishes with rabbit, grouse and venison on the menu.
Police in Surrey are on the case of a violent gang who steal millions of pounds worth of jewellery, and Avon and Somerset police tackle guns being sent through the post from Miami.
The National Crime Agency - Britain's answer to the FBI - tackle a people-smuggling ring, a Ghanaian cocaine smuggling operation, and organised online romance fraud.
The Stakes Are High: In Perth, the pressure is on for a team tackling a high-stakes engine rebuild, and in Townsville, a crew of experts build a massive underframe for a freight powerhouse.
Monterey to San Diego: Rick Stein travels down the Californian coastline immortalised by John Steinbeck, past citrus groves and vineyards that inspired one of his favourite films, and into LA.
Officers hand over a convict to Mexican authorities in the middle of the Juarez Lincoln bridge, and a man drops a marijuana joint on a customs floor in Progreso.
A man fears for his life after being stopped by a cartel in Laredo, clubbers in Detroit are found with drugs disguised in liquid, and officers stop a shipment of frozen rats.
Guy Martin helps with the restoration of Birmingham Botanical Gardens while learning how to blow glass and trying to rebuild one of the first lawnmowers.
The rucking great motoring show. Jeremy and James enjoy a game of rugby without leaving their driving seats, and Lewis Hamilton swaps his F1 wheels for a Reasonably Priced Car.
More motoring mayhem. Jeremy and Richard tackle the interconnected issues of an ageing population and the complexity of modern cars by designing a vehicle for pensioners.
Food Pairings: Si and Dave explore how British ingredients can combine to make classic pairings. The duo create a pork and apple dish and a pear and stilton tart.
San Diego to Baja California: Rick enjoys a hearty steak-and-eggs American diner special before heading across the reputedly busiest border crossing in the world - San Diego to Tijuana.
More Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material with guests including Charlie Brooker, Johnny Marr and Shazia Mirza.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Our survey says... that former Family Fortunes host Les Dennis is joining actor Charlie Brooks, comedian Darren Harriott, and TV presenter Melinda Messenger on this edition of the show.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs for a look back at 2022.
New. Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of other guests as he continues to try to make sense of the bewildering times we live in.
Ulex: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Bridget Christie, Rosie Jones, Cariad Lloyd and Alan Davies to find out about ulex and other obscure 'U' words.
